video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: This video will walk you through how to use the budget versus actual spreadsheet. This spreadsheet is a powerful tool.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: I know it might look a bit full on at first glance, but trust me, it's simpler than it seems.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Let me show you how to use it and how it can help you make smart decisions in your business.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: First, why this spreadsheet matters. This tool helps you run your own. What if analysis? That means you can test different scenarios like hiring a

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: new team member or buying a piece of equipment and see the impact before you commit. Usually your accountant does this kind of thing,

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: but with this spreadsheet, you can do it yourself anytime. For example, thinking about hiring someone, pop their costs into the sheet.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Copy that across a few months and you'll see what extra income or profit you'll need to make that higher worthwhile.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: You can also look at how well your current team is being used. Set targets for productivity, estimate marketing and sales goals,

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: and check if you're getting the most out of what you already have. It also works beautifully alongside your cashflow forecast

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: and break even tools. Right up top, you'll see the sheet is called profit and loss. This is based on when invoices are issued,

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: not when money hits the bank. That's the key difference from your cashflow forecast, which tracks actual payments in and out.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Think of this as a month by month preview of what you want your finances to look like. It's kind of like a budget but on steroids.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Step one, build your budget. Start by grabbing your profit and loss report for the last 12 months. It doesn't have to match the financial year,

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: just the most recent full year. Even better. If you've got two years of solid data, use that now. Work out the average monthly amount for each income

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: and expense line in your business. Let's say residential work brought in $20,000 a month on average. You'd plug that into each month in the budget column.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Then move on to commercial or subcontracting work materials and supplier costs, subcontractors, equipment hire, and your regular business expenses.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Use the left hand column of the spreadsheet as a guide, and if the labels don't match your business, no worries.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: You can change them to suit. Once you've filled out the whole year, the spreadsheet will show your estimated profit for each month.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Scroll across to the far right and you'll see your total projected profit for the year. Ask yourself, does this look like the kind of profit I want?

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: If not, here's where the what if comes in. Want to increase your profit? Try bumping up your residential work and see what changes.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Use this sheet to test those ideas and see what it would take to make them happen. Step two, enter your actuals.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Now, each month after your accounts are up to date, grab your accrual base, profit and loss report, not cash based.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: If you can avoid it. Enter your actual numbers into the spreadsheet, what you actually made, what you spent on materials,

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: your real expenses like software or vehicle fuel. This lets you compare your actual results to your budgeted targets. You'll be able to see where you're tracking well,

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: where you've overspent, Where your income might need a push, um, and you can use this alongside your financial dashboard

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: to spot trends, make adjustments and stay on track. A few final tips, download the spreadsheet and have a go.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: You do not need to get it perfect the first time. If you get stuck, jump into the trad preneur group and ask.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: Other members will likely have done it before, and our team is always around to help. So take the pressure off to start with version one.

video_name: How to Use the Budget vs Actual Spreadsheet
text: It'll only get better from there.
